data.title: Asiatic Group reiterates claims over Cambodian subsidiary
data.description:
Colben Energy is a material component of the group.

Embattled Asiatic Group Holdings Limited posted in a bourse filing that it continues to look for an amicable resolution over the control of its Cambodian subsidiary Colben Energy (CEZ).

This response comes in response to questions raised by a shareholder in relation to the company’s annual report for the financial year ended 31 March 2025. A shareholder has asked Asiatic Group to clarify whether it still intends to pursue an amicable resolution to the dispute with its Cambodian joint venture partner, or if it will continue allocating resources to ongoing arbitration proceedings.

The group reiterated that it has taken steps to do so, including seeking mediation through the Singapore Mediation Centre on 8 November 2024.

“Unfortunately, until today, the parties are not able to agree on a common ground. The company is always open to amicable resolution of the dispute based on terms and conditions acceptable to both parties and not based on terms and conditions dictated solely by one party,” the group said.

In responding to queries of its shareholders, the group added in their announcement, “The Colben Entities, who are parties to an ongoing arbitration, are subject to confidentiality obligations that only permit limited disclosures of information pertaining to the Arbitration. The Colben Entities and the company will seek and be guided by the legal advice in relation to the information that can and should be disclosed.”

Asiatic shared that during a general meeting on 14 March 2024, the other shareholder of CEZ attempted to remove one of the representatives of the company as a director and chairman of CEZ and to remove the same representative as the authorised signatory of CEZ’s bank accounts. The group is challenging these acts.

data.title: Union Steel inks agreements for regional oil and gas expansion
data.description:
The projects may include offshore construction for oil and gas installations.

Union Steel Holdings Limited has signed cooperation agreements with COSCO SHIPPING Marine Engineering and China Offshore Engineering Solutions for regional oil and gas expansion projects.

The projects may include offshore construction for oil and gas installations, decommissioning platforms in Southeast Asia, international ocean towing and heavy lift transportation, amongst others.

“By pooling our engineering expertise, R&D capabilities, and regional market access, this collaboration strategically positions us to offer integrated and cost-efficient solutions and tap into new growth opportunities within Southeast Asia’s oil & gas markets,” Ang Yu Seng, Executive Chairman and CEO of Union Steel, said.

data.title: MPACT sells 2 Japan office buildings for $78.7m
data.description:
After sale, MPACT’s portfolio will comprise 15 commercial properties across Asia.

Mapletree Pan Asia Commercial Trust (MPACT), through its subsidiary Tsubaki Tokutei Mokuteki Kaisha, sold two office buildings in Japan to two unnamed parties.

ABAS Shin-Yokohama (ASY), a nine-storey office building located in Yokohama City, will be sold at a sale price of $30m (JPY3,330m), and TS Ikebukuro Building (TSI), a single-tenanted nine-storey office building in Tokyo, will be sold at a sale price of $48.7m (JPY5,400m).

The combined divestment price amounts to $78.7m (JPY8,730m), representing a 1.7% premium (in local currency terms) against the aggregate purchase price of $104.1m (JPY8,580m). The divestment consideration for both properties will be satisfied wholly in cash.

According to MPACT’s bourse filing on 23 July, the proposed divestments reflect its ongoing portfolio reconstitution efforts and demonstrate its disciplined approach to portfolio and capital management. The divestment proceeds will be directed towards debt reduction to further enhance MPACT’s balance sheet resilience.

The divestments are scheduled for completion by end-August FY25/26 and upon completion of the divestments, MPACT’s portfolio will comprise 15 quality commercial properties located across the five key gateway markets of Asia.

data.title: Microsoft launches first Southeast Asia lab with AI focus
data.description:
The move is aimed at deepening the company’s artificial intelligence (AI) research, industry collaboration, and talent development efforts.

Microsoft has launched a new research facility under its Microsoft Research Asia division in Singapore, marking the company’s first such lab in Southeast Asia.

The move is aimed at deepening the company’s artificial intelligence (AI) research, industry collaboration, and talent development efforts in the region.

The establishment of the lab is supported by the Singapore Economic Development Board (EDB) and expands Microsoft’s global research network.

According to a media statement issued by Edelman, the lab will act as a strategic hub to drive AI initiatives with a focus on foundational research, responsible deployment, and socially beneficial applications.

The lab plans to collaborate with local institutions and apply advanced AI across sectors such as healthcare, finance, and logistics.

Notably, it will work with SingHealth to promote precision health, and with the National University of Singapore (NUS) and Nanyang Technological University (NTU) to explore embodied AI.

Efforts will also include developing AI systems tailored to Southeast Asia’s cultural and societal contexts.

Microsoft also emphasized the lab’s role in cultivating local talent, with partnerships involving NUS, NTU, and the Singapore Management University (SMU) to provide students and researchers with hands-on experience in AI development.

data.title: Creator-led retail space opens in Dempsey Hill
data.description:
Reverie On Hill (ROH) positions itself as Singapore’s first creator-powered retail space that offers shoppers incentives

A new retail concept focused on content-driven customer engagement has opened in Dempsey Hill.

Reverie On Hill (ROH), which launches on 25 July, positions itself as Singapore’s first creator-powered retail space that offers shoppers incentives to share product reviews and social content in exchange for discounts, perks, and affiliate income, according to creative agency Damnzai.

Located at 8D Dempsey Road, ROH features more than 200 curated products across skincare, wellness, and lifestyle categories.

Instead of charging brands listing fees, the agency said ROH uses real-time customer feedback and content performance to determine which products remain on shelves. Products that consistently underperform are rotated out on a quarterly basis.

Each product in-store is tagged with a QR code allowing customers to learn more, post reviews, or generate personal affiliate links. Customers who drive a sale through their content are eligible for commission, the agency said.

data.title: Singapore scam losses surge 70% with $1.1b in 2024 fraud
data.description:
69% of banking executives in Asia Pacific now consider scams and mule accounts to be the dominant fraud concern.

Scam-related losses in Singapore surged to $1.1b (US$860m) in 2024, a 70% increase over the previous year, according to an industry poll.

According to FICO’s latest Asia Pacific fraud survey, which drew insights from more than 40 fraud and risk leaders across the region, 69% of banking executives in Asia Pacific now consider scams and mule accounts to be the dominant fraud concern—surpassing traditional forms of unauthorized transactions.

The survey also flagged social media as the top external threat channel, cited by 52% of respondents, followed by messaging apps at 35%.

These platforms—widely used in Singapore—are frequently exploited by syndicates impersonating officials, advertising fake jobs, or promoting fraudulent investment schemes. Many users are also unknowingly recruited to “rent out” their accounts, aiding criminal networks in laundering stolen funds.

Operationally, banks face internal hurdles in their fraud response capabilities. Siloed data (46%), lack of integration across products and channels (28%), and limited third-party system connectivity (13%) were the most common barriers cited by survey respondents.

Views on consumer reimbursement for scams remain split. Only 14% of executives support full reimbursement in all scam cases, while 50% believe compensation should only apply when the bank is at fault.

Another 36% support a shared liability model, reflecting ongoing debate across the region about how to fairly distribute responsibility between institutions and customers.

data.title: Moomoo Singapore surpasses 1.5 million app users
data.description:
The investment app has launched an AI investment assistant.

Moomoo Singapore, a financial broker, said that its app moomoo has surpassed 1.5 million users, a 50% increase in user growth within a 15 month period.

Moomoo offers a digitalised brokerage and wealth management platform, offering investment products via its app.

CEO Gavin Chia said that nearly 7 in 10 clients of Moomoo were profitable in the past 12 months.

On a whole-of-portfolio basis, equities remain the most popular instrument for investors, forming nearly 60% of a typical winning portfolio. Wealth management products also saw significant allocation at 26%, Chia said in a keynote address during a company event held on 12 July 2025 at Suntec Convention Centre.

“When sentiment around the US market shifted, many investors took a proactive approach: trimming US allocations from 75% to 67%, increasing their exposure to Hong Kong from 9% to 12%, and maintaining a stable position of around 20% in Singapore,” Chia said.

Moomoo also launched Moomoo AI during the event, touted as an advanced investment assistant that is powered by cutting-edge LLM technology. It can help users analyse stocks and track market trends through a simple, conversational interface.

Moomoo AI delivers real-time, holistic insights by combining financial data, technical indicators, and the latest news, all tailored to each query, the company said.

Beyond AI, Moomoo said that it is doubling down on digital assets to build on the successful rollout of crypto trading in 2024. This made Moomoo Singapore the first and only online broker licensed to offer crypto trading services in the city.

data.title: OUE REIT's distribution per unit climbs 5.4% YoY in H1
data.description:
The growth reflects the resilience of its diversified Singapore portfolio.

 

OUE Real Estate Investment Trust reported that its distribution per unit (DPU) increased by 5.4% YoY to 0.98 Singapore cents for the first half of 2025. 

 

Excluding the capital distribution, core DPU also increased by 11.4% YoY.

 

This gain reflects effective capital management and the resilient performance of its Singapore commercial portfolio, it said in a report on 23 July. 

 

OUE REIT reported that its Singapore office properties’ committed occupancy remained healthy at 95.5% with positive rental reversion of 9.1% achieved in Q2 2025. Mandarin Gallery occupancy also remained high at 99.0% with positive rental reversion of 34.3% recorded in the same period. 

 

In its report, it said that revenue and net property income (NPI) for H1 2025 were $131.1m and $105.3m, representing YoY declines of 10.6% and 10.1% respectively, mainly due to the absence of revenue contributions from Lippo Plaza Shanghai, which was divested in December 2024.

 

On a like-for-like basis, revenue and NPI slightly decreased by 2.7% and 2.0% YoY, respectively, with the Singapore commercial portfolio performance partially offsetting lower contributions from the hospitality segment. 

 

Share of joint venture results increased by 41.0% YoY to $6.3m for H1 2025.

 

Unitholders can expect the distribution payment for H1 2025 on 3 September, after the books are closed on 31 July.

data.title: Singapore inflation eases with broad price moderation in H1
data.description:
The Consumer Price Index (CPI)-All Items for general households rose by 0.9% on a YoY basis from January to June 2025.

Singapore’s consumer price growth moderated in the first half of 2025, with inflation easing across all household income groups, according to figures released by the Singapore Department of Statistics on 23 July 2025.

The Consumer Price Index (CPI)-All Items for general households rose by 0.9% on a YoY basis from January to June 2025, down from the 1.8% increase recorded in the second half of 2024. All income groups experienced slower inflation over the same period.

By household income group, CPI-All Items inflation stood at 0.8% for both the lowest 20% and middle 60% income groups, and 1.2% for the highest 20%.

When excluding imputed rentals for owner-occupied housing, the CPI rose by 0.6% for the lowest income group, 0.7% for the middle group, and 1.3% for the highest income group.

The Department identified Accommodation, Health Insurance, Food, and Motor Cars as the main contributors to the CPI increase across all household groups. These upward pressures were partially offset by lower prices for Holiday Expenses and Electricity.

The highest 20% income group experienced the largest increase in CPI excluding imputed rentals, primarily due to a higher expenditure share on motor cars, which had a stronger inflationary effect on their consumption basket.

Category-level data showed that food prices rose by around 1.2% to 1.3% across income groups. Health and transport costs also increased, whilst prices declined in categories such as Clothing and Footwear, Information and Communication, Recreation, and Education.

Notably, education expenses fell by as much as 2.8% for the lowest income group.

data.title: Temus supports AI adoption with AWS and DISG partnership
data.description:
Temus, a digital transformation firm headquartered in Singapore, has partnered with Amazon Web Services (AWS) and Digital Industry Singapore (DISG) to support the rollout of the AWS AI Springboard initiative.

The program is designed to help up to 300 Singapore-based enterprises adopt and scale artificial intelligence (AI) solutions, with eligible businesses receiving up to $600,000 in cloud credits and training support.

As part of the initiative, Temus will provide consulting services to guide organisations from early-stage AI discovery to implementation and ongoing support.

The firm’s approach is aimed at reducing adoption complexity and cost, whilst enabling businesses to integrate AI technologies into core operations to improve productivity, customer service, and operational resilience.
